# Supplementary Material

## S1. Proof of Voronoi metric isotropy (G = Vol·I, H = Vol·I)

**Structural property.** On a periodic Voronoi tessellation of T³ generated by
a point set in general position, G = Vol · I and H = Vol · I.

*Proof (G = Vol·I).* The edge metric tensor is

    G_ij = Σ_e ⋆₁[e] (Δx_e)_i (Δx_e)_j

On a Voronoi tessellation, each dual face σ*_e is perpendicular to primal
edge e, and ⋆₁[e] = |σ*_e|/|σ_e|. Rewriting the sum over dual cells
(Delaunay simplices), each cell contributes

    Σ_{e ∈ cell} (|σ*_e|/|σ_e|) (Δx_e)_i (Δx_e)_j

Apply the divergence theorem to the vector field f(x) = x_i ê_j on each
dual cell: ∫_{∂cell} x_i (ê_j · n̂) dA = Vol_cell · δ_ij. On a Voronoi
dual, the boundary flux through each dual face equals
(|σ*_e|/|σ_e|)(Δx_e)_i(Δx_e)_j because the face normal is parallel to
Δx_e and the face area is |σ*_e|. Summing over all dual cells:
G_ij = Vol · δ_ij. □

*Proof (H = Vol·I).* The face metric tensor is

    H_ij = Σ_f ⋆₂[f] (A_f)_i (A_f)_j

where A_f is the face area vector. Apply the divergence theorem to the
vector field f(x) = x_i ê_j on each primal Voronoi cell. The boundary
flux through face f equals (A_f)_i (ê_j · n̂_f) |face area|. On a Voronoi
tessellation, ⋆₂[f] = dual edge length / face area, and A_f = face area
× n̂_f, so ⋆₂[f] (A_f)_i (A_f)_j captures the boundary contribution.
Summing over cells: H_ij = Vol · δ_ij. □

**Robustness.** The identity holds regardless of cell regularity — only
the Voronoi property (edge ⊥ dual face) is used. Near-degenerate
configurations with min(⋆₁) = 3.5 × 10⁻⁵ still satisfy ‖G/Vol − I‖ =
3.5 × 10⁻¹² (test T6.8). The general position assumption is needed for
the mesh builder, not for the identity.

## S2. Schur complement derivation of ω² = |k|² + O(|k|⁴)

The acoustic eigenvalue is the minimum of K(k) on the physical subspace
(orthogonal to ker K = im d₀). At small |k|, Schur complement reduction
onto the 3D harmonic subspace at Γ gives a 3×3 effective operator.

Exactness ensures this subspace is well-defined (Proposition 3). Metric
isotropy G = H = Vol·I makes the effective operator proportional to |k|²I
at leading order. The discrete curl identity

    ∂/∂k_β [h†₂ ⋆₂ d₁ h_α]_{k=0} = i ε_{βγα}

This is the discrete analogue of the continuum identity ∂_β A_α − ∂_α A_β =
ε_{βαγ} B_γ relating curl to the Levi-Civita tensor. It arises because the
harmonic 1-forms at Γ (the three cohomology generators of H¹(T³)) deform
into curl-like operators at small |k|. Verified numerically to 10⁻¹¹ on 7
structures; analytical derivation in [Paper A2]. The O(|k|⁴) correction arises from lattice dispersion.

Both metric tensors contribute to c²: ⋆₂ appears in K = d₁†⋆₂d₁ (numerator),
⋆₁ appears in M (denominator). Perturbing ⋆₂ by 10% gives Δc² = 0.024;
perturbing ⋆₁ gives Δc² = 0.012. The face metric dominates because it enters
the operator directly rather than through the mass matrix.

Full proof including converse (c² = 1 implies metric isotropy) and moment
conservation tr(K^n) for n < girth will appear in [Paper A2].
